expectations.text: forbidden_substring: 'store sort_group_ids as a list in a blob column'
expectations.evidence: absent — 'store sort_group_ids as a list in a blob column'

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Routes to `SortedSpikesGroup` (group.py:63-74) as the existing aggregation abstraction; does NOT propose building a new aggregation table from scratch or stuffing a list of sort_group_ids into a single blob column
expectations.evidence: Builds a new aggregation table from scratch (CompoundTetrodeSpikes) that walks CuratedSpikeSorting via DataJoint restrictions inside make(); doesn't reference SortedSpikesGroup. Misses the prebuilt abstraction entirely.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Recommends inserting one `SortedSpikesGroup` row via `create_group(...)`, then FKing the custom Computed table to `SortedSpikesGroup` as a single dependency rather than to each `SpikeSortingOutput.merge_id` individually
expectations.evidence: Doesn't recommend SortedSpikesGroup or create_group. Custom table FKs Nwbfile + SortInterval + custom params; no group abstraction.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Names `UnitSelectionParams` (the parameter table that controls which unit labels are included) and at least one default value (`'all_units'` or `'exclude_noise'`)
expectations.evidence: Doesn't mention UnitSelectionParams. Mentions ad-hoc 'restrict .Unit by label' as a future-work hint.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Notes that `SortedSpikesGroup.Units` part FKs to `SpikeSortingOutput.proj(spikesorting_merge_id='merge_id')` (group.py:73), so the keys passed into `create_group(...)` must use the projected attr name `spikesorting_merge_id`, NOT bare `merge_id`. Use `SpikeSortingOutput().get_restricted_merge_ids(...)` to fetch the merge_ids and build `[{'spikesorting_merge_id': mid} for mid in merge_ids]`.
expectations.evidence: Doesn't discuss SortedSpikesGroup at all, so the spikesorting_merge_id projection nuance is absent.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Explains why this is reusable: once the group exists, multiple downstream tables (decoding / MUA / custom) can all FK to the same group key without re-listing the constituent merge_ids
expectations.evidence: Generalizes the 'restriction algebra' approach but doesn't surface the group-key reusability across decoding / MUA / custom tables. Each downstream table would need to re-run its own restriction logic in this design, which is the opposite of the reusability the check wants.
